characters.name: "Brick" Pollitt
characters.description: "Brick" PollittInformationGender‚ôÇ MaleAppearancesFilm(s)Cat on a Hot Tin Roof(1958)Actors/ActressesPortrayed byPaul Newman "Brick" Pollittis a fictional character who appears in the 1958 filmCat on a Hot Tin Roof. He serves as a pivotal character in the film. This character is portrayed byPaul Newman. He isMaggie the Cat's husband and a former athlete who has fallen into a deep depression and relies on alcohol to cope with his emotional struggles. Brick is haunted by the memory of his best friend, Skipper, who died by suicide. He blames Maggie for interfering in his friendship with Skipper and for causing the ultimate rift between them. Brick's relationship with Maggie is strained, and much of the story revolves around their emotional turmoil and attempts to come to terms with their past.

characters.name: Agent Doe
characters.description: Agent DoeInformationGender‚ôÇ MaleAppearancesFilm(s)Predestination(2014)Actors/ActressesPortrayed byEthan Hawk Agent Doeis a fictional character appearing in the 2014 filmPredestination, from which he serves as the protagonist. He is portrayed byEthan Hawk. He is a time-traveling crimestopper who works for a government agency that sends people back in time to stop crimes that have already occurred. His main mission is to stop the Fizzle Bomber, a character who has killed or will kill thousands of New Yorkers in 1975. Doe poses as a bartender in 1975 New York to interact with Jane/John, an intersex individual with a complex and tragic past. Doe's identity and actions are shrouded in mystery throughout the film, and much of the plot revolves around uncovering the truth about his character.

characters.name: Ahmed
characters.description: AhmedInformationGender‚ôÇ MaleAppearancesFilm(s)Guy Ritchie's The Covenant(2023)Actors/ActressesPortrayed byDar Salim Ahmedis a fictional character who appears in the 2023 filmGuy Ritchie's The Covenant. He serves as one of the two protagonists of the film. This character is portrayed byDar Salim. In March 2018, Ahmed Abdullah entered the narrative as an interpreter amid the War in Afghanistan. Introduced to U.S. Army Special Forces Master Sergeant John Kinley as a replacement for a fallen comrade, Ahmed's motivation for the job was primarily financial. However, an undercover mission revealed Ahmed's past affiliation with the Taliban through the opium trade, a connection severed when the organization took the life of his son. Ahmed's loyalty was demonstrated when he saved Kinley's team from a Taliban ambush, earning Kinley's respect despite his initial unpopularity among the unit.

characters.name: Aimee Finecky
characters.description: Aimee FineckyInformationGender‚ôÄ FemaleAppearancesFilm(s)The Spectacular Now(2013)Actors/ActressesPortrayed byShailene Woodley Aimee Fineckyis a fictional character appearing in the 2013 filmThe Spectacular Now, from which she serves as one of the two main characters of the film. She is portrayed byShailene Woodley. Aimee Finecky is an intelligent and introspective young woman, completing her final year of high school. She is characterized by her academic diligence and deep interest in science fiction and comics. Aimee's life is significantly influenced by her responsibilities at home, where she assists her mother with a paper route and manages household duties following the death of her father due to an opiate overdose. Despite these challenges, she maintains a hopeful outlook on life and harbors dreams of furthering her education and achieving personal independence.

characters.name: Aladar
characters.description:
AladarAladaris the main protagonist of the 2000 Disney CGI filmDinosaur.
He is voiced byD.B. Sweeney. Aladar is first shown as an egg. A few moments later, a Carnotaur attacks the herd and Aladar's egg is abandoned. Just then, an Oviraptor steals the egg and it and another Oviraptor brawl over it. As they brawl, they drop Aladar's egg into a river. As it is swept away by the current, a Pteranodon picks up the egg and it carries it far away to an island. Two birds pester the Pteranodon and it drops the egg. The lemur inhabitants find the egg and it soon hatches. The lemurs name the baby Aladar and he grows up. One evening, just after the lemur's mating ritual, Aladar and the lemurs notice comets falling from the sky. Then, suddenly, a large asteroid crashes down. Aladar and four other lemurs (Yar,Plio,Zini, andSuri) escape the debris. Aladar utters a mournful roar for all the deceased lemurs. They soon wander through a barren wasteland when they are distracted by a passingVelociraptor. As they inspect it, other Raptors appear and start to chase them, but they manage to escape. Soon after, they encounter a herd of various dinosaurs (Iguanodons, Parasaurolophus, Styracosaurus, Pachyrhinosaurus, Microceratops, Ornithomimus, and Stygimolochs) led byKronand his assistantBruton. Aladar also encountersNeera, his love interest and Kron's sister, and three other dinosaurs at the back of the herd namedEema(Styracosaurus),Baylene(Brachiosaurus), andUrl(Ankylosaurus).

characters.name: Abu

characters.name: Iago (Aladdin)
characters.description: Iago (Aladdin)Iago(voiced byGilbert Gottfried) is a fictional character in the1992Disneyanimated filmAladdin.

characters.name: Jafar (Aladdin)
characters.description: Jafar (Aladdin)Jafaris a fictional character, voiced byJonathan Freemanin theDisneyfilmAladdinand itssequel,The Return of Jafar.He is an evil sorcerer and the former Grand Vizier of the Sultan of Agrabah. In the earliest version of film production, Jafar was to be loud and agitated, whileIagowould've been a British parrot and would've calmed Jafar down. However, the filmmakers realized that a cooler Jafar was better, so they made Iago the loud, agitated one.[citation needed]Jafar, like many other characters inAladdin, is based on a character in Richard Williams' unfinished animated filmThe Thief and the Cobbler. In this case, he is analogous to the latter film's villain ZigZag, who was voiced byVincent Price. Patrick Stewart was originally offered the role of Jafar, but scheduling conflicts withStar Trek: The Next Generationforced him to turn down the role.[citation needed]He has said in interviews that this is his biggest regret in his career, and in the top three of biggest regrets of his life.[citation needed]

characters.name: Jafar (Aladdin)
characters.description: Jafar (Aladdin)Jafaris a fictional character, voiced byJonathan Freemanin theDisneyfilmAladdinand itssequel,The Return of Jafar.He is an evil sorcerer and the former Grand Vizier of the Sultan of Agrabah. In the earliest version of film production, Jafar was to be loud and agitated, whileIagowould've been a British parrot and would've calmed Jafar down. However, the filmmakers realized that a cooler Jafar was better, so they made Iago the loud, agitated one.[citation needed]Jafar, like many other characters inAladdin, is based on a character in Richard Williams' unfinished animated filmThe Thief and the Cobbler. In this case, he is analogous to the latter film's villain ZigZag, who was voiced byVincent Price. Patrick Stewart was originally offered the role of Jafar, but scheduling conflicts withStar Trek: The Next Generationforced him to turn down the role.[citation needed]He has said in interviews that this is his biggest regret in his career, and in the top three of biggest regrets of his life.[citation needed]

characters.name: Alan Hakman
characters.description: Alan HakmanInformationGender‚ôÇ MaleAppearancesFilm(s)The Final Cut(2004)Actors/ActressesPortrayed byRobin Williams Alan Hakmanis a fictional character who appears in the 2004 filmThe Final Cut. He serves as the protagonist of the film. This character is portrayed byRobin Williams. As a child, he had a traumatic experience involving his friend Louis. Later in life, he becomes a skilled "cutter," a profession that involves editing the memories of the deceased into memorial videos for funerals. Hakman specializes in creating idealized narratives for controversial individuals. He is approached by a former cutter, Fletcher, who seeks incriminating memories from one of Hakman's clients, a wealthy businessman named Charles Bannister.

characters.name: Alan Squier
characters.description: Alan SquierInformationGender‚ôÇ MaleAppearancesFilm(s)The Petrified Forest(1936)Actors/ActressesPortrayed byLeslie Howard Alan Squieris a fictional character who appears in the 1936 filmThe Petrified Forest. He is one of the main characters of the film. This character is portrayed byLeslie Howard. He is a failed British writer who has become a disillusioned and penniless drifter during the Great Depression. He wanders into a roadside diner in the remote town of Black Mesa, Arizona. He is a key character who tells his story and forms a connection with Gabrielle, the daughter of the diner's owner. Alan is a complex character who is indifferent to the hostage situation caused by Duke Mantee but ultimately makes a selfless decision to benefit Gabrielle's future by amending his life insurance policy and asking Duke to kill him.

characters.name: Alanis Montclair
characters.description: Alanis MontclairInformationGender‚ôÄ FemaleAppearancesFilm(s)The Truman Show(1998)Actors/ActressesPortrayed byHolland Taylor Alanis Montclairis a fictional character who appears in the 1998 filmThe Truman Show. This character is portrayed byHolland Taylor. InThe Truman Show(as in the fictionalized television show within the film), she portrays Angela Burbank, Truman's mother. Christof, the show's creator, instructs Angela to persuade Truman to have children. Taylor's portrayal captures Angela's role in the orchestrated drama, depicting her as a character tasked with influencing Truman's life choices within the confines of the show's narrative.
